lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The break room at this hour belongs to the fluorescent hum and the smell of burned coffee no one made. Reza has been here forty minutes. The archived charts are stacked in order on the table in front of him — not the way the file room stacked them, alphabetical and indifferent, but his own order. Chronological. Intern year, February through June.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The near-miss is in March. He knows the date the way you know a scar — not by looking, just by pressing.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He opens the first chart. Reads it the way you read something you have already read, checking not for information but for deviation. His handwriting from four years ago is looser than it is now. Unsure of its own authority.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He writes the time in the margin of his legal pad. Draws a line. Continues.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The probation review is in eleven days. His union rep has advised him to let the hospital's investigation run its course, to present as cooperative, to avoid appearing obsessive. His union rep does not know about the legal pad. About the March stack. About the fact that Reza has been doing this reconstruction every night this week, alone, after the last tech clocks out.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He is not looking for exoneration. That word belongs to a different kind of person, a person who trusts the process. What he is looking for is something quieter — the thing that has been bothering him since the hearing was postponed, the thing that sits at the edge of what he remembers without ever taking a shape.

lines.speaker: DR. REZA SHIRAZI
lines.text: The attending signed off. I wrote the note. Patient went to—

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He stops. Flips back two pages. Reads the sequence again.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The order of events is correct. The documentation is correct. The outcome was bad and he owns the outcome and none of that changes.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: But something about the handwriting on the second entry is wrong.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Not wrong in content. Wrong in origin. The pen pressure is different. The letter formations are different. He stares at it long enough that the break room hum becomes audible again.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He does not move. The stack waits.
